:::{#def-breslow-baseline-cuhaz-est}
#### Breslow estimator of the baseline cumulative hazard

$$\hat \cuhaz_0(t) \eqdef
\sum_{t_i \le t} \frac{d_i}{\sum_{k\in R(t_i)} \hazfactor(\vx_k)}$$

where, for the distinct event times $t_1 < \cdots < t_K$ of the data set:

- $d_i$ is the number of events at $t_i$;
- $R(t_i) \eqdef \set{j : Y_j \ge t_i}$ is the risk set at $t_i$ (where $Y_j = \min(T_j, C_j)$ is subject $j$'s observed follow-up time) — the subjects still under observation just before $t_i$;
- $\hazfactor(\vx) \eqdef \expf{\tp{\vx}\vb}$ is the hazard ratio (risk score) for covariate pattern $\vx$.

:::

:::{#exm-breslow-baseline-cuhaz-est}
#### Breslow estimator with two event times

Suppose there are two distinct event times $t_1 = 2$ and $t_2 = 4$ (no ties, $d_1 = d_2 = 1$),
with risk-set-weighted totals $\sum_{k \in R(2)} \hazfactor(\vx_k) = 10$
and $\sum_{k \in R(4)} \hazfactor(\vx_k) = 6$.
Then

$$
\hat\cuhaz_0(4) = \frac{1}{10} + \frac{1}{6} \approx 0.267 .
$$
:::
